app.title: CloudEasyFiles
settings.download_directory_helper: This should be the root folder path where the system will create the mirrored cloud file structure to replicate and store downloaded files using the same local hierarchy.
settings.download_directory_notice_inval: The configured location is not valid. The "Download" option will remain unavailable until a valid path is configured. Even so, you can still use "Download As" to save the file to another location.
settings.download_directory_notice_missi: No local mapping path is configured. The "Download" option will remain unavailable until you configure a valid path. Even so, you can still use "Download As" to save the file to another location.
settings.download_directory_pick_failed: Unable to open the folder picker.
hero.subtitle: A simple interface for navigating cloud connections, exploring resources, and exposing contextual actions based on the selected item.
navigation.title: Connections
navigation.connection_status.unsupported: This provider does not have real connectivity implemented yet.
navigation.empty.title: No connections configured
navigation.empty.description: Start by adding a connection to populate the left navigation tree.
navigation.filter.label: Filter navigation items
navigation.filter.empty: No navigation items match the current filter.
navigation.modal.title: Configure connection
navigation.modal.load_error: Unable to load the selected connection.
navigation.modal.credentials_load_warnin: Stored credentials could not be loaded. Re-enter them to update this connection.
navigation.modal.aws.access_key_placehol: Enter the AWS access key ID
navigation.modal.aws.secret_key_placehol: Enter the AWS secret access key
navigation.modal.aws.restricted_bucket_h: Optional. When set, this connection is limited to that bucket and can work without `s3:ListAllMyBuckets`.
navigation.modal.aws.connect_on_startup_: When this option is enabled, the app will try to connect to this provider automatically during startup.
navigation.modal.aws.cache_directory_hel: When configured, downloaded files are tracked locally so the app can show file status.
navigation.modal.aws.upload_tier_helper: Choose the storage class used for new uploads made through this connection in the app. This setting does not change objects that are already stored in AWS.
navigation.modal.aws.test_connection_hel: Runs a real AWS validation using the credentials provided above.
navigation.modal.aws.test_connection_in_: Testing AWS credentials and configured S3 access...
navigation.modal.aws.test_connection_suc: Connection test succeeded. AWS account detected: {accountId}.
navigation.modal.aws.test_connection_mis: The AWS credentials are valid, but this identity cannot list S3 buckets. Configure a specific bucket to use this connection without `s3:ListAllMyBuckets`, or update the AWS user's permissions to add `s3:ListAllMyBuckets`.
navigation.modal.aws.test_connection_res: This connection is restricted to a specific bucket and the operation tried to access a different bucket.
navigation.modal.aws.test_connection_val: Fill the required AWS fields before testing the connection.
navigation.modal.validation.restricted_b: Enter a valid AWS bucket name.
navigation.modal.azure.placeholder_copy: Azure-specific settings are not implemented yet. For now, use AWS connections.
navigation.modal.validation.connection_n: Use only simple names with letters, numbers, spaces, hyphens, or underscores, up to {max} characters.
A connection with this name already exists.
aws.upload_tier.label: AWS upload tier
aws.upload_tier.helper: Choose the storage class used for new uploads made through this connection in the app. This setting does not change objects that are already stored in AWS.
aws.upload_tier.note_body: The comparisons below are qualitative and can vary by region, volume, minimum retention, and retrieval charges. Check the official table at
aws.upload_tier.note_docs_body: For complete behavior and eligibility details for each storage class, see
aws.change_tier.label: New AWS tier
aws.change_tier.helper: Choose the new AWS storage class. This changes how the file is stored and can affect price and availability.
aws.change_tier.note_title: What can change when you change tier
aws.change_tier.note_body: Some tiers cost less to store, but may charge for retrieval, require minimum retention, or make access less immediate. Read more at
aws.change_tier.note_docs_body: For a clearer overview of the official AWS options, see
aws.storage_class.standard.title: S3 Standard
aws.storage_class.standard.use_case: For frequent access with low latency and high availability across multiple Availability Zones.
aws.storage_class.standard.cost: Highest storage cost, without infrequent-access savings.
aws.storage_class.standard_ia.title: S3 Standard-Infrequent Access
aws.storage_class.standard_ia.use_case: For data accessed less often but still needing rapid access.
aws.storage_class.standard_ia.cost: Lower storage cost than Standard, with retrieval charges and minimum storage duration.
aws.storage_class.onezone_ia.title: S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access
aws.storage_class.onezone_ia.use_case: For infrequently accessed data that can live in a single Availability Zone.
aws.storage_class.onezone_ia.cost: Lower cost than Standard-IA, with less resilience and retrieval charges.
aws.storage_class.intelligent_tiering.us: For data with unpredictable access patterns, allowing automatic movement across tiers.
aws.storage_class.intelligent_tiering.av: Immediate access for frequent/infrequent tiers; archive tiers require restore.
aws.storage_class.intelligent_tiering.co: Avoids overcommitting to one class, but adds monitoring and automation charges.
aws.storage_class.glacier_ir.title: S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval
aws.storage_class.glacier_ir.use_case: For long-term archive data that still needs immediate millisecond retrieval.
aws.storage_class.glacier_ir.availabilit: Immediate millisecond access for rarely accessed archived data.
aws.storage_class.glacier_ir.cost: Low storage cost with retrieval charges and archive-style minimum duration.
aws.storage_class.glacier.title: S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval
aws.storage_class.glacier.use_case: For long-term archives where restore times of minutes or hours are acceptable.
aws.storage_class.glacier.availability: Archived objects; restore required before normal use.
aws.storage_class.glacier.cost: Very low storage cost, with meaningful restore and minimum-retention tradeoffs.
aws.storage_class.deep_archive.title: S3 Glacier Deep Archive
aws.storage_class.deep_archive.use_case: For lowest-cost long-term retention with very rare access.
aws.storage_class.deep_archive.availabil: Deep archive; restore is typically slower.
aws.storage_class.deep_archive.cost: Lowest storage cost, with slower restores and longer minimum retention.
aws.restore_tier.expedited.title: Expedited
aws.restore_tier.expedited.use_case: Use when the file is urgent and retrieval speed matters more than cost.
aws.restore_tier.standard.title: Standard
aws.restore_tier.standard.use_case: Use for most restores when you want a practical balance between speed and cost.
aws.restore_tier.bulk.title: Bulk
aws.restore_tier.bulk.use_case: Use for large or non-urgent retrievals when cost matters more than speed.
navigation.connections.delete_descriptio: This will remove the saved connection "{name}" from the application. The {provider} account and the files stored there will not be deleted.
navigation.connections.delete_error: Unable to delete the selected connection.
home.title: Home
content.empty.title: Select a connection
content.empty.description: Once connections are configured, the selected item details will appear here.
content.connection.description: This saved connection stores access configuration and provider-specific defaults, while credentials are kept in secure OS storage and the local mapping path is configured globally in the app.
content.container.description: This simulated container represents a real S3 bucket or Azure blob container at the root of the cloud hierarchy.
content.directory.description: This simulated folder is based on object-storage prefixes and matches the folder navigation shown by the product.
content.file.description: This simulated file represents a cloud object with normalized storage, availability, and local mapping state.
content.view_mode.label: Content view mode
content.filter.label: Filter visible content items
content.filter.empty: No visible items match the current filter.
content.list.title: Contents
content.list.loading: Loading items from the selected container...
content.list.loading_containers: Loading buckets for the selected connection...
content.list.connect_to_load: Connect this connection to view its buckets.
content.list.empty_connection: This connection does not have buckets available in the current session.
content.list.empty_container: This container does not have immediate items at the current level.
content.list.empty_directory: This folder does not have immediate items at the current level.
content.list.load_error: Unable to load items for the selected context.
content.folder.name_helper: This folder will be created in "{path}".
content.folder.create_failed: Unable to create the folder in the current path.
content.delete.description_files: This will permanently remove {count} selected file items from the current bucket.
content.delete.description_recursive: This will permanently remove {files} selected files and recursively delete {folders} selected folders, including content that may not be loaded in the current view.
content.delete.confirmation_instruction: Type {value} to confirm this permanent action.
content.delete.confirmation_mismatch: Type the confirmation value exactly to continue.
content.delete.failed: Unable to remove the selected items.

content.transfer.download_as_failed: Unable to export this file to the selected destination.
content.transfer.upload_failed: Unable to upload this file to the current folder.
content.transfer.upload_picker_failed: Unable to open the file picker for upload.
content.transfer.upload_invalid_path: The selected local file path is invalid.
content.transfer.upload_duplicate_active: An upload for this destination is already in progress.
content.transfer.upload_duplicate_batch: This selection contains more than one file targeting "{name}". Only the first one will be uploaded.
content.transfer.upload_single_file_only: Simple upload accepts only one file at a time.
content.transfer.upload_drop_path_unavai: This drag-and-drop payload did not expose a usable local file path.
content.storage_class_change.title: Change tier for {count} file(s)
content.storage_class_change.intro: This operation changes the file's AWS storage class. The selected tier can affect price, retrieval charges, and how quickly the file is available.
content.storage_class_change.confirmatio: You are changing the tier of {count} file(s) to {storageClass}.
Archived objects must be restored before this operation. Some tiers reduce storage cost, but may increase retrieval cost or make access less immediate.
content.storage_class_change.choose_dest: Choose a destination tier before continuing.
content.storage_class_change.same_class_: Choose a storage class that differs from the current class.
content.storage_class_change.submit_fail: Unable to change the tier of the selected files.
content.storage_class_change.tooltip_arc: Archived objects must be restored before changing tier.
content.storage_class_change.tooltip_sel: All selected items must be available AWS files to change tier.
content.transfer.upload_overwrite_confir: A file named "{name}" already exists in this folder. Replace it?
content.transfer.conflict_modal_body: Choose how to handle this duplicate before continuing the upload batch.
content.transfer.download_as_completed_f: The file was saved to the selected destination.
content.local_cache.not_configured: No global local mapping path configured
restore.modal.generic.title: Restore archived file
restore.modal.generic.placeholder: This provider-specific restore flow has not been implemented yet.
restore.modal.aws.title: Restore archived S3 object
restore.modal.submit_failed: Unable to submit the restore request for this archived file.
restore.modal.aws.tier_helper: Speed and request cost vary by tier.
restore.modal.aws.days_helper: After this period, the temporary restored copy expires and the object returns to its archival tier.
restore.modal.aws.days_validation: Choose a retention period between 1 and 365 days.
restore.modal.aws.cost_note_body: AWS pricing depends on storage class, retrieval tier, requested volume, region, and temporary restored storage. The estimates below are advisory only.
restore.modal.aws.retention_note_body: Retention days affect how long the temporary restored copy stays available and may affect temporary storage cost, but they do not redefine the retrieval request fee itself.
restore.modal.aws.confirmation_summary: You are requesting an AWS {tier} restore and asking S3 to keep the temporary restored copy available for {days} day(s).
restore.modal.aws.confirmation_summary_b: You are requesting an AWS {tier} restore for {count} files and asking S3 to keep the temporary restored copies available for {days} day(s).
restore.modal.aws.confirmation_warning: Restored files are not downloaded automatically. You must monitor the restore progress and download the file before the configured availability window expires.
